channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Gen Z, desperate to get off their phones, is powering a sewing renaissance
item.description: Other traditional textile art like crocheting, knitting, and embroidery have popped off among Gen Zers.

item.title: Bitcoin stabilizes after tariff whiplash briefly sends price below $65,000
item.description: The latest volatility for the original cryptocurrency follows the Supreme Court’s landmark decision on Friday.

item.title: Sam Altman is tired of ‘unfair’ critiques about AI’s energy use. Climate experts say his defensive stance is misguided
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has defended the resource-intensive use of AI by comparing it to all the energy—and food—that humans require, sparking a wave of...

item.title: The Number of Robots and AI Agents Will Soon ‘Explode,’ Outpacing Human Workers
item.description: The prediction about robots and AI agents comes from the head of innovation at Citi Global Insights.

item.title: This Controversial Fundraising Hack Is Booming—but It Might Not Be Smart for Your Startup
item.description: A new report found that some AI startups are using a multitiered fundraising maneuver to inflate their valuations. Here’s how it works.

item.title: 100 major housing markets with falling home prices
item.description: Want more housing market stories from Lance Lambert’s ResiClub in your inbox? Subscribe to the ResiClub newsletter . Based on our analysis of the Zill...

item.title: The Pentagon wants fewer AI limits. Anthropic doesn’t. Here’s why it matters
item.description: Dario Amodei , CEO of Anthropic, will head to the Pentagon on Tuesday to meet with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth about how the military uses the comp...

item.title: In less than a year, Trump erased 12 years of solvency for the trust fund that pays for Medicare Part A
item.description: The CBO estimates that benefit reductions would start at 8% in 2040 and steadily climb to a 10% cut by 2056.

item.title: Micron’s $100 billion New York semiconductor manufacturing project is facing local pushback
item.description: On a snowy Friday in January, dignitaries from both political parties braved the chill of a central New York winter for the groundbreaking ceremony of...

item.title: ‘Maya MacGuineas should be ashamed’: Scott Bessent launches war of words with the nonpartisan think tank that agrees with Trump on tariffs
item.description: For her part, MacGuineas said Bessent’s diatribe was “a bit of an odd response.”

item.title: This Single ChatGPT Prompt Can Transform Your Important Emails—and Get 10x Better Results
item.description: Catherine Goetze, known as CatGPT, says a simply structured prompt can take ChatGPT from light editor to strategic thinking partner.

item.title: AI agents that do your work while you sleep sound great. The reality is far messier—‘it’s like a toddler that needs to be overseen’
item.description: Early adopters using tools like OpenClaw and Claude Code say always-on AI is improving—but experts say they are still far from totally hands-off.

item.title: Experts Warn of Unknown Dangers in Viral Peptide Craze
item.description: Without clinical trials, it’s not clear whether the peptides are safe.

item.title: Private Equity Is Finally Recovering—but Investor Returns Just Hit a Record Low
item.description: A new report from Bain & Company has the good news—and the bad.

item.title: Strategy keeps adding to a losing bitcoin position as prices drop
Dow now on track for its worst day in a month as Trump plans replacement tariffs
Dow shedding over 850 points as stocks' sharp losses deepen in final hour — live
Men yell at AI in all caps 80% more than women
item.description: As AI use continues to grow, so is frustration with the technology. From strange responses that don’t make any sense to learning curves to how it’s im...

item.title: Don’t Short Yourself: Sign up now for Wednesday’s free, live tax-advice event
PayPal shares pop on takeover hopes. Here’s who could swoop in with a purchase.
